title: An excerpting engine tasked with mapping this corpus must be calibrated to recognize the profound me...
description: An excerpting engine tasked with mapping this corpus must be calibrated to recognize the profound methodological frameworks that governed the original authors

title: The engine must intelligently evaluate pronouns, demonstratives, and opening conjunctions, identifyi...
description:
The engine must intelligently evaluate pronouns, demonstratives, and opening conjunctions, identifying causal particles while maintaining "forgiving retention" to prevent unsafe starts at causal continuations.1 Furthermore, it must distinguish between the objective mapping of scholarly disagreement (tahrir al-khilaf) and the subjective assertion of a preferred conclusion (tarjih), ensuring that attribution remains rigorously accurate.1
This comprehensive report addresses four critical open questions regarding the computational parsing and excerpting of classical Islamic texts

title:
Semantic Boundaries and School-Specific Distinction Thresholds
A primary and enduring challenge in p...
description:
Semantic Boundaries and School-Specific Distinction Thresholds
A primary and enduring challenge in processing comparative Islamic jurisprudence (fiqh muqaran) computationally lies in determining when school-specific definitions of a single technical term warrant entirely separate, distinct excerpt entries within a database, versus when they should be merged and tagged merely as stylistic variants within a single comprehensive entry

title: The algorithmic threshold for separating definitions must not rely on simple string-matching or surf...
description: The algorithmic threshold for separating definitions must not rely on simple string-matching or surface-level lexical divergence

title: Instead, it must be predicated upon an analysis of the semantic scope—the inclusive and exclusive bo...
description: Instead, it must be predicated upon an analysis of the semantic scope—the inclusive and exclusive boundaries of the definition—and whether that scope alters the concrete legal application or outcome (thamarat al-khilaf)

title: If the boundaries of the definition dictate different legal rulings, liabilities, or validations, th...
description: If the boundaries of the definition dictate different legal rulings, liabilities, or validations, the engine must generate distinct definition excerpts

title: If the boundaries encompass the exact same logical set of realities, the engine must collapse the de...
description: If the boundaries encompass the exact same logical set of realities, the engine must collapse the definitions into a single object with variant properties

title: The first calibration case concerning الغصب (Ghasb / Usurpation) reveals a situation where the termi...
description: The first calibration case concerning الغصب (Ghasb / Usurpation) reveals a situation where the terminological difference is far from a mere matter of phrasing; it fundamentally dictates the scope of legal liability (dhaman) and penal consequence.2 The Hanafi text utilizes the highly restrictive phrase أخذ مال متقوم (the taking of legally valued, tangible property) and further stipulates the physical condition على وجه يزيل يده (in a manner that removes the owner's hand or physical possession).3 Consequently, in Hanafi jurisprudence, usurping real estate (which cannot be physically moved or carried away) or abstract, intangible rights (such as a right of way or intellectual property equivalents) does not technically constitute Ghasb in the strictest penal sense, thereby altering the mechanism of restitution.4 Conversely, the Shafi'i definition employs the broader, more abstract phrase الاستيلاء على حق الغير (domination over the right of another).4 By utilizing the concept of "domination" (istila') rather than "taking" (akhdh), and "right" (haqq) rather than "tangible property" (mal), the Shafi'i definition comprehensively absorbs real estate, abstract rights, and properties lacking classical legal value in specific contexts (like a trained hunting dog or certain types of manure).3 Because the inclusionary and exclusionary zones of these definitions dictate entirely different jurisprudential outcomes in court, they mathematically cross the threshold into requiring strictly separate excerpt entries in any legal database

title: A user querying the definition of Ghasb must be presented with two distinct ontological nodes
description: A user querying the definition of Ghasb must be presented with two distinct ontological nodes

title: The Hanafis strictly compartmentalize civil financial competence from moral probity, defining incomp...
description: The Hanafis strictly compartmentalize civil financial competence from moral probity, defining incompetence solely through the secular lens of financial squandering (tabdhir) and the inability to manage material assets, explicitly stating that a sinner who manages money well is not a Safih.6 Because the definition determines the suspension of human civil rights, these must be treated by the engine as independent conceptual definitions rather than mere variations

title: In contrast, the stylistic variant cases demonstrate where the engine must synthesize and merge
description: In contrast, the stylistic variant cases demonstrate where the engine must synthesize and merge

title: In the definitions of النكاح (Nikah), the comparative text reveals a purely rhetorical divergence ro...
description: In the definitions of النكاح (Nikah), the comparative text reveals a purely rhetorical divergence rooted in the metaphorical constraints of the Arabic language and the desire for legal euphemism.7 The Hanafi use of ملك المتعة (ownership of enjoyment) and the Shafi'i use of ملك وطء (ownership of intercourse) are distinct lexemes pointing to the identical objective, physical, and legal reality.8 Classical commentators explicitly note that this is a khilaf lafzhi, meaning the variation exists to satisfy linguistic precision regarding the metaphor of the word Nikah—which literally translates in pre-Islamic Arabic to either physical conjunction or the contract itself—and not to alter the legal limits of the marriage contract.8 An advanced excerpting engine must recognize synonymous syntactical anchors through semantic embedding algorithms and merge these into a single definition entry

title: The variations should be tagged as school-specific phrasing rather than conceptual distinctions, pre...
description: The variations should be tagged as school-specific phrasing rather than conceptual distinctions, preventing database bloat and clarifying to the researcher that the schools are in absolute agreement on the substantive reality of the contract

title: The engine should maintain a single overarching definitional excerpt or parent node for "The Poor an...
description: The engine should maintain a single overarching definitional excerpt or parent node for "The Poor and Needy (Recipients of Zakat)" while structurally delineating the inverted sub-categorizations as child nodes

title: A dynamic "significance test" is required to determine whether a brief mention embedded within a lar...
description: A dynamic "significance test" is required to determine whether a brief mention embedded within a larger discussion possesses sufficient semantic independence to warrant its own discrete excerpt, or if it is merely contextual connective tissue that must remain locked within the parent excerpt to preserve coherence

title: The explanation of derivational paronomasia (Tajnis al-Ishtiqaq) is highly valuable for linguists an...
description: The explanation of derivational paronomasia (Tajnis al-Ishtiqaq) is highly valuable for linguists and must be isolated

title: It must remain to preserve the primary quotation
description: It must remain to preserve the primary quotation

title: The current foundational principles governing the engine dictate that excerpts must maintain speaker...
description: The current foundational principles governing the engine dictate that excerpts must maintain speaker-role correctness and resolve implied dependencies (taqdir), requiring "forgiving retention" to prevent unsafe starts at causal continuations like li'anna (because) or fa'inna (for indeed).1 When observing the cases above, the baseline criteria of structural independence and thematic density hold true, but they are insufficient for fully automated, high-fidelity parsing of classical Arabic

title: To bridge this gap, an excerpting engine must incorporate additional classical criteria that scholar...
description: To bridge this gap, an excerpting engine must incorporate additional classical criteria that scholars historically utilized to judge the weight and boundaries of a digression

title: The first additional criterion is Istiqlal al-Mabna wa al-Ma'na (Absolute Structural and Semantic In...
description: The first additional criterion is Istiqlal al-Mabna wa al-Ma'na (Absolute Structural and Semantic Independence)

title: The algorithmic parser must ensure that the dependency tree of the extracted sentence has no unresol...
description: The algorithmic parser must ensure that the dependency tree of the extracted sentence has no unresolved external roots

title: The engine should utilize named entity recognition (NER) and topic modeling to detect micro-shifts i...
description: The engine should utilize named entity recognition (NER) and topic modeling to detect micro-shifts in domain vocabulary; a sudden influx of rhetorical terms within a legal text is a primary indicator of an extractable Istidrad

title: The third criterion is Al-Bina' 'ala Asl Mustaqill (Foundation on an Independent Proof)
description: The third criterion is Al-Bina' 'ala Asl Mustaqill (Foundation on an Independent Proof)

title: Finally, the engine must look for Qasd al-Ifadah wa al-Tanbih (The Explicit Intent to Benefit)
description: Finally, the engine must look for Qasd al-Ifadah wa al-Tanbih (The Explicit Intent to Benefit)

title: The presence of scholarly meta-markers such as فائدة (Benefit), تنبيه (Alert), تذنيب (Appendix), or ...
description: The presence of scholarly meta-markers such as فائدة (Benefit), تنبيه (Alert), تذنيب (Appendix), or فرع (Branch) is a direct, explicit instruction from the original author that the upcoming text, however brief, contains concentrated, standalone value.21 While Case 5 demonstrated that this marker alone is not absolute (as it must be paired with semantic independence), it serves as a highly reliable weighting factor for the engine's significance scoring algorithm

title:
The Context-Fill Threshold and Principles of Pedagogical Summarization
In classical Islamic texts, a...
description:
The Context-Fill Threshold and Principles of Pedagogical Summarization
In classical Islamic texts, a recurring methodological phenomenon is the sophisticated use of Ihalah (cross-referencing or referral), manifested through standardized phrases such as تقدم ذكره ("its mention has preceded") or سيأتي في باب ("it will come in the chapter of...").26 When an excerpt carries related text from another excerpt—for example, when a secondary ruling heavily relies on a primary foundational proof established earlier in the volume—the excerpting engine faces the "context-fill threshold." The fundamental question arises: at what point should the carried text be replaced by a modern summary or hyperlink pointer instead of algorithmically injecting the full original text into the new excerpt

title: To resolve this, the engine must be modeled upon the historical realities of classical Islamic schol...
description: To resolve this, the engine must be modeled upon the historical realities of classical Islamic scholarly pedagogy

title: When a scholar is establishing a primary foundational ruling (Asl) or addressing a highly contentiou...
description: When a scholar is establishing a primary foundational ruling (Asl) or addressing a highly contentious issue where the precise wording of the evidence dictates the strict boundaries of the law, the full text is invariably provided

title: Instead, the text will simply state: وقد تقدم في باب الربا ("And the proofs for this have preceded i...
description:
Instead, the text will simply state: وقد تقدم في باب الربا ("And the proofs for this have preceded in the chapter of Usury").26 Similarly, when dealing with the highly specific rules of a follower in congregational prayer (Ma'mum) forgetting a mandatory pillar, the text will briefly summarize the governing rule established earlier regarding the Imam bearing the burden of the follower's mistake, rather than reproducing the foundational arguments in full.30
The decision to provide full text versus a summary reference is governed by three traditional jurisprudential and pedagogical principles, which must be encoded into the excerpting engine's operational logic:
1

title: In algorithmic terms, if replacing a carried text with a context-fill pointer strips the excerpt of ...
description:
In algorithmic terms, if replacing a carried text with a context-fill pointer strips the excerpt of its necessary conditions (shurut) or preventative boundaries (mawani'), the context-fill threshold has not been met, and the full text must be retained to ensure the excerpt achieves the "Study-Ready" grade mandated by the engine's foundational principles.1
2

title: If the excerpt in question is a branch, the engine should confidently replace the carried foundation...
description: If the excerpt in question is a branch, the engine should confidently replace the carried foundational text with a summary pointer or hyperlink

title: If the original text utilizes explicit referencing terminology (taqaddama, saya'ti), the engine must...
description: If the original text utilizes explicit referencing terminology (taqaddama, saya'ti), the engine must honor the author's intentional omission and utilize a pointer

title: Pre-excerpt structural analysis must inevitably inform the algorithmic mapping, but it must absolute...
description: Pre-excerpt structural analysis must inevitably inform the algorithmic mapping, but it must absolutely not dictate or rigidly decide the final excerpting outcome, because the nominal classification is frequently misleading

title: However, the great later commentator Ibn Abidin, in his masterful supercommentary Radd al-Muhtar, im...
description: However, the great later commentator Ibn Abidin, in his masterful supercommentary Radd al-Muhtar, immediately confronts and corrects this taxonomic boundary.32 He notes that the discussion of the Caliphate technically and epistemologically belongs to Ilm al-Kalam (scholastic theology) and Siyasah Shar'iyyah (public and constitutional law).32 The only reason it was placed in the chapter of ritual prayer by earlier jurists is due to associative historical sequencing: the Prophet Muhammad explicitly appointed Abu Bakr to lead the ritual prayer during his final illness, which the Companions later used as the primary analogical proof (Qiyas) for his right to the political Caliphate.32 Ibn Abidin acknowledges the historical logic of the placement but firmly re-classifies the content conceptually for his readers

title: To handle this boundary tension accurately, classical scholars utilized a layered approach, which mu...
description:
To handle this boundary tension accurately, classical scholars utilized a layered approach, which must be mirrored by the computational logic of an advanced excerpting engine:
1

title: Algorithms must similarly preserve the exact hierarchical path of the excerpt (e.g., Book of Prayer ...
description: Algorithms must similarly preserve the exact hierarchical path of the excerpt (e.g., Book of Prayer > Chapter of Leading > The Supreme Caliphate) to maintain historical fidelity and allow researchers to trace the origin of the text

title: They used boundary-correcting phrases such as وهذا موضعه كتاب البيوع ("And the proper structural pla...
description: They used boundary-correcting phrases such as وهذا موضعه كتاب البيوع ("And the proper structural place for this is the Book of Financial Transactions") or وهذا من مباحث علم الكلام ("And this is from the discussions of scholastic theology").32 The engine must allow a content-derived taxonomy—driven by semantic analysis and keyword density—to override the inherited structural taxonomy when generating modern metadata tags for the excerpt

title: The excerpting engine must evaluate the content of the passage to determine its ultimate category, e...
description: The excerpting engine must evaluate the content of the passage to determine its ultimate category, ensuring that a modern researcher querying "Constitutional Law in Hanafi Fiqh" successfully retrieves the excerpt on the Caliphate, despite it being structurally housed under "Ritual Prayer"
